Module com.example._0tazo
Package com.example._0tazo.model

Class Table

java.lang.Object
com.example._0tazo.model.Table
public class Table extends Object
Represents the physical table in the Cincuentazo game.

Responsibilities:

  • Tracking the current table sum.
  • Placing the initial card that starts the game.
  • Receiving cards played by players and updating the sum.
  • Exposing the top card of the discard pile for the UI.

Table holds a reference to the shared Deck so it can send played cards to the discard pile directly, keeping Game focused on turn flow and player management.

See Also:
  • Game
  • Deck

  • Constructor Details

    • Table

      public Table(Deck deck)
      Constructs a Table with a reference to the shared deck. The table sum starts at zero until placeInitialCard() is called.
      Parameters:
      deck - the shared Deck; must not be null

  • Method Details

    • placeInitialCard

      public void placeInitialCard() throws GameException
      Deals one card face up from the deck and places it on the table, initializing the table sum with that card's value.

      Per the game rules, the initial card determines the starting sum:

      • 9 â†’ sum starts at 0
      • A â†’ sum starts at 1 (since currentSum is 0, getValue returns 10; however, per the rules the initial A counts as 1 â€” handled here)
      • J, Q, K â†’ sum starts at âˆ’10
      • 2â€“8, 10 â†’ sum starts at their face value
      Throws:
      GameException - if the deck has no cards available

    • placeCard

      public void placeCard(Card card)
      Places a card on the table and updates the sum.

      The card's value is added to the current sum. This method does not validate whether the sum would exceed 50 â€” that check is the caller's responsibility via Card.isPlayable(int) before calling this method.

      Parameters:
      card - the Card to place on the table; must not be null

    • isCardPlayable

      public boolean isCardPlayable(Card card)
      Returns true if playing the given card would keep the table sum at or below 50.

      Delegates to Card.isPlayable(int) with the current sum.

      Parameters:
      card - the card to check
      Returns:
      true if the card is legally playable

    • getTableSum

      public int getTableSum()
      Returns the current table sum.
      Returns:
      the table sum

    • getTopCard

      public Card getTopCard()
      Returns the top card of the discard pile â€” the last card played on the table â€” without removing it.
      Returns:
      the top Card, or null if the discard pile is empty

    • isAtLimit

      public boolean isAtLimit()
      Returns true if the current sum has reached or exceeded the maximum allowed value of 50.
      Returns:
      true if the table is at its limit

    • addToSum

      public void addToSum(int value)
      Adds a value directly to the table sum without going through Card.getValue(int).

      Used exclusively when the human player manually selects the Ace's value (1 or 10) via the overlay, bypassing the automatic value resolution in Card.getValue(int).

      Parameters:
      value - the integer value to add to the table sum; must be either 1 or 10 when called for an Ace
